name: Aider
description: AI pair programming in your terminal. Works directly with Git, proposing commits instead of raw code changes. Connects to multiple AI models.
supported_models: Claude, GPT-5, Gemini, DeepSeek, Llama, and 30+ models via API
pricing_note: Free and open source; pay for model API usage
notes: One of the earliest AI CLI tools. Git-native workflow — all changes are proposed as commits.

name: Amazon Q Developer CLI
description: AI coding assistant for command-line workflows, optimised for AWS. Helps with AWS CLI commands, infrastructure as code, and cloud best practices.
notes: Best for AWS-heavy workflows. Deep integration with AWS services, IAM, CloudFormation.

name: Claude Code
description: Agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal. Understands your entire codebase, handles multi-file edits, runs commands, and manages git — all through natural language.
supported_models: Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Haiku 3.5
pricing_note: Included with Claude Pro ($20/mo), Max ($100/mo), or API usage
notes: Pioneer of the agentic CLI category. $1B+ ARR. Deep integration with Claude models for autonomous task completion.

name: CodeRabbit CLI
description: AI code review tool in the terminal. Context-aware feedback, line-by-line suggestions, and best practices enforcement.
pricing_note: Free for open source; Pro from $15/user/mo
notes: Focused on code review rather than code generation. Integrates with CI/CD pipelines.

name: Codebuff
description: Terminal-first coding assistant that makes changes inside real repos. Context-aware with "knowledge" files for repo conventions.
pricing_note: Free tier with limits; Pro from $20/mo
notes: Knowledge files keep it aligned with repo conventions. No IDE dependency.

name: Codex CLI
description: Multi-agent coding CLI with parallel execution. Runs multiple AI agents simultaneously, each on an isolated Git worktree.
pricing_note: Included with ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo), Pro ($200/mo), or API usage
notes: Multi-agent parallelism with isolated worktrees. Each agent works on its own branch.

name: Crush
description: LSP-enhanced, MCP-extensible AI coding agent with the broadest platform support. Beautiful terminal UI built on the Charm ecosystem.
pricing_note: Free and open source; pay for model API usage
platforms: macOS, Linux, Windows, Android, FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD
notes: Broadest platform support of any CLI tool. Mid-session model switching. LSP integration.

name: Firecrawl CLI
description: Web data toolkit for AI agents. Scrapes, searches, and browses the web from the terminal. Turns websites into clean LLM-ready markdown.
supported_models: Tool — works with any AI agent (Claude Code, Codex, etc.)
notes: Not a coding agent — a web data tool for agents. Scrape, search, crawl, browse. FIRE-1 autonomous agent.

name: Gemini CLI
description: Terminal-first AI coding assistant with the largest context window. Brings Gemini models into the shell for code generation, refactoring, and multi-file reasoning.
supported_models: Gemini 2.5 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Gemini 2.0 Flash
pricing_note: Generous free tier with Gemini API; paid via Google AI Studio
notes: Largest context window (1M tokens) — 5x Claude Code, 8x Codex. Ideal for large codebases.

name: Goose
description: Fully open-source AI agent that goes beyond code suggestions. Executes full development workflows — building, running, debugging, and orchestrating multi-step tasks.
pricing_note: Free and open source; pay for model API usage
notes: Co-designed MCP with Anthropic. Block backing gives enterprise credibility. Desktop app + CLI.

name: Plandex
description: AI coding agent that breaks large tasks into structured plans and executes them step by step. Massive effective context with Tree-sitter indexing.
notes: 2M token effective context via Tree-sitter indexing. Plan-first approach to large tasks.

name: Qwen Code
description: Command-line AI coding agent adapted from Gemini CLI, optimised for Qwen3-Coder models. 480B parameter MoE architecture.
pricing_note: Free and open source; use with local or API models
notes: Based on Gemini CLI architecture. Optimised for Qwen3-Coder (480B MoE). Fully free.

name: Warp
description: Modern terminal that blends GPU-accelerated UI with AI assistance and multi-agent orchestration. Runs Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI within the same interface.
supported_models: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI + own agents
notes: Meta-tool: orchestrates multiple AI agents in one terminal. Written in Rust, GPU-accelerated.
